title: nepeta ludlow-hewittii
intro: Nepeta ludlow-hewittii, also known as the Himalayan catmint, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae). Traditionally used in herbal medicine, it has been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating a variety of health conditions. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and alleviating symptoms of digestive issues. In herbal preparations, Nepeta ludlow-hewittii can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to reap its medicinal benefits.
